mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and the requirement for specific resource allocations.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is essential for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command fetches the credentials for your cluster.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on the cluster
steps.description:
This command deploys the Kagenti application on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> This command installs Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment kagenti --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace kagenti

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml --namespace kagenti

```

> This command sets up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Ensure you have a monitoring configuration file ready.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Kagenti deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti Helm release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This process includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> This command upgrades the Kagenti installation.
> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name to allow scheduling again.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1' to reset kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Helm chart version is incorrect or the repository is not added.

**Fix:**
Ensure the repository is added with 'helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti' and try again.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application is failing to start, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kagenti' to diagnose.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when the cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up your nodes or adjust resource requests in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
